event: Laceration to the right lower extremity
Increased edema, erythema, and pain to the affected leg
Drained the lesions resulting in the expression of clear fluid and sloughing of the epidermis
Erythematous and edematous right lower extremity with ecchymosis, superficial erosions, and clear drainage
Vital signs were within normal limits
WBC count of 7400 cells/mcL with 50% band neutrophils
Admitted to the hospital for cellulitis of the right lower extremity, bandemia, and lactic acidosis
Acute hypoxic respiratory failure with altered mentation and hypotension
Transferred to the Intensive Care Unit secondary to severe sepsis
Antimicrobial susceptibilities displayed the organism was sensitive to ceftriaxone, ciprofloxacin, ertapenem, and meropenem
Ciprofloxacin was added to her treatment regimen
Ciprofloxacin was discontinued due to concerns for Clostridium difficile infection
One month course of 2 g ceftriaxone IVPB administered daily
